cards.back: The Cartesian product of two sets A and B, denoted as A x B, is the set of all ordered pairs (a, b) where a is an element of A and b is an element of B.

cards.front: What is the definition of a Relation?
cards.back: A relation between two sets A and B is a subset of the Cartesian product A x B, representing a connection or mapping between elements of A and elements of B.

cards.front: What is the difference between a Function and a Relation?
cards.back: A function is a special type of relation where each input element (or first component of the ordered pair) corresponds to exactly one output element (or second component of the ordered pair). A relation is a more general concept that only requires one input element to be mapped to exactly one output element, but it may not be the case for all input elements.

cards.front: What is the Domain of a Relation?
cards.back: The domain of a relation R between sets A and B is the set of all input elements (first components of the ordered pairs) in R, i.e., {a | (a, b) ∈ R for some b ∈ B}.

cards.front: What is the Range of a Relation?
cards.back: The range of a relation R between sets A and B is the set of all output elements (second components of the ordered pairs) in R, i.e., {b | (a, b) ∈ R for some a ∈ A}.
